metadata.description: Comprehensive Edo-English dictionary for the BiZY Edo Translator app
metadata.author: BiZY Language Data

entries.example_english: How are you? I am fine.

entries.english: I am fine / It is good
entries.example_english: How are you? I am fine, thank you.
My father has gone to the market.
My body is well / I feel fine.

entries.example_edo: Ọsan ọ ghale vbe ọ dẹ.
entries.example_english: The orange is ripe and sweet.

entries.example_edo: Ẹdẹ ne a ya ọ ghẹ dẹ.
Ẹdẹ ne ọ ya ima ya.

entries.example_english: The river has no water (is dry).
The leopard is in the bush.
The crocodile lives in the river.
A snake is in the bush.
The chief went to the king's palace.

entries.english: Benin City (city of the Edo people)
God willing / If it is God's will

entries.example_edo: Uhunmwun ọ gha ọre, ọmwan ọ gha rrọọ.
entries.example_english: If you use your head wisely, you will survive.

entries.edo: Ẹkpẹn ọ gha khẹ ẹhe ọre ọ gha gbe
entries.english: The leopard that roars in the night frightens the brave
entries.pronunciation: eh-kpehn oh ghah kheh eh-heh oh-reh oh ghah gbeh
entries.example_edo: Ẹkpẹn ọ gha khẹ ẹhe, ọre ọ gha gbe ọmwan ọghe iran.
entries.example_english: A person's reputation speaks for them even before they arrive.

entries.edo: Ọmwan ọ gha ya ọlọ ọ gha de otẹ
entries.english: One who goes to the market will return with news
entries.pronunciation: oh-mwahn oh ghah yah oh-loh oh ghah deh oh-teh
entries.example_edo: Ọmwan ọ gha ya ọlọ ọ gha de otẹ.

entries.example_english: Take the plate and give me food.
The bird sits on top of the tree.
It went up to the sky.

entries.english: It is good / It is alright
entries.example_english: It is alright, let us go.

entries.english: It hurts me / I am in pain
I am happy / I love it
It is powerful / It is strong

entries.example_english: This person went to the market.

entries.english: It is not there / There is none
It is there / There is some

entries.example_edo: Ọ gha ya, ẹghẹ ọ ghẹ rrọọ.
entries.example_english: Let's go, there is no time.

phrases.edo: Ọ vbi mwẹn ọ miẹ ẹyin.
phrases.english: I am happy to meet you.
phrases.pronunciation: oh vbee mwehn oh myeh eh-yeen

phrases.english: My body is not well / I am sick.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ọ gha rẹn ẹre, ọre gha miẹ ẹvbo ẹre.
proverbs.english: He who knows himself will find his way.

proverbs.edo: Ẹwẹ ọ ghẹ rrọọ vbe ọọn ne ọ ẹmwẹn.
proverbs.english: Water does not stay in a pot that is broken.

proverbs.edo: Ọba ọ gha yan ọ gha ya, ọmwan ọ gha rẹn ẹre ne ọ gha miẹ.
proverbs.english: When the king gives a command, those who understand will benefit.

proverbs.edo: Ima kẹre iran, ẹhọ iran ọ de.
proverbs.english: When we are united, great prosperity comes.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ọ ghẹ rẹn iye ẹre, ọ ghẹ rẹn egbe ẹre.
proverbs.english: He who does not know his mother does not know himself.

proverbs.edo: Ẹkhọe ọ kpolo, ugbẹ ọ ghẹ khuẹn.
proverbs.english: In a large forest, a goat does not get lost.

proverbs.edo: Ẹghẹ ọ ya, ọmwan gha miẹ irẹn.
proverbs.english: As time passes, one finds truth.

proverbs.edo: Ọmwan ọ gha ya ọlọ vbe ẹrhẹn ọ gha de otẹ.
proverbs.english: He who goes to the market at night returns with news.

lessons.title: Basic Greetings
lessons.description: Learn how to greet people in Edo at different times of day.

lessons.title: Pronouns and Basic Sentences
lessons.description: Learn Edo pronouns and how to form simple sentences.

lessons.title: Family Members
lessons.description: Learn vocabulary for family relationships in Edo culture.

lessons.title: The Body
lessons.description: Learn the names of body parts in Edo.

lessons.title: Food and Drink
lessons.description: Learn Edo words for common foods, drinks, and ingredients.

lessons.title: Numbers 1-20
lessons.description: Count in Edo from one to twenty and beyond.

lessons.title: Time Expressions
lessons.description: Talk about time, days, and schedules in Edo.

lessons.title: Essential Verbs
lessons.description: Master the most common action words in Edo.

lessons.title: Describing Things
lessons.description: Learn adjectives and how to describe size, color, and quality.

lessons.title: Animals
lessons.description: Learn animal names important to Edo culture.

lessons.title: Edo Proverbs and Wisdom
lessons.description: Explore the rich proverb tradition of the Edo people.

lessons.title: Religion and Culture
lessons.description: Understand Edo spiritual and cultural vocabulary.
